stimulus: According to horticulturists, an apple tree infested with fire blight may develop wilted, blackened shoots that resemble burned twigs. A ______blank apple tree, on the other hand, will produce shoots that are firm and uniformly green from new growth to leaf tip.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice C is the best answer because "thriving" means flourishing and free of disease, which is precisely the contrast the text sets up with infested trees. Choice A is incorrect because "typical" describes commonness rather than health; the text focuses specifically on disease status, not how usual the tree is. Choice B is incorrect because "productive" describes yield, not disease state. Choice D is incorrect because "weakened" suggests an unhealthy tree, the opposite of what the contrast requires.
